title: Keevash–Mubayi–Sudakov–Verstraëte Question on Rainbow Cycles
statement_text: What is the maximum possible average degree of a properly edge-coloured graph on $n$ vertices containing no rainbow cycle?
context_text: Keevash, Mubayi, Sudakov, and Verstraëte raised this question in 2007 as a rainbow analogue of the classical fact that average degree at least 2 guarantees a cycle. The lower bound $\Omega(\log n)$ was already established in [39]; prior to this paper the best upper bound was $O(\log^2 n)$, due independently to Janzer–Sudakov and Kim–Lee–Liu–Tran.
paper_contribution: Proves an upper bound of $(\log n)^{1+o(1)}$ on the maximum average degree, matching the $\Omega(\log n)$ lower bound up to the $o(1)$ term in the exponent and essentially resolving the question.
paper_title: Essentially tight bounds for rainbow cycles in proper edge-colourings
